input.prompt:
I'm building a photo viewer page in my .NET MAUI app targeting .NET 10. I want
the image to fill the entire screen edge-to-edge, going behind the status bar
and navigation bar. But my image still has padding around it even though I set
SafeAreaEdges="None" on the ContentPage. What am I missing?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm building a photo viewer page in my .NET MAUI app targeting .NET 10. I want
the image to fill the entire screen edge-to-edge, going behind the status bar
and navigation bar. But my image still has padding around it even though I set
SafeAreaEdges="None" on the ContentPage. What am I missing?

data.content:
`SafeAreaEdges="None"` tells MAUI not to add safe-area padding, but you likely also need to:

**1. Hide the navigation bar** (Shell or NavigationPage chrome still reserves space):

```xml
<!-- If using Shell -->
<ContentPage Shell.NavBarIsVisible="False"
             SafeAreaEdges="None">

<!-- If using NavigationPage -->
<ContentPage NavigationPage.HasNavigationBar="False"
             SafeAreaEdges="None">
```

**2. On Android, opt into edge-to-edge rendering.** The system bars still occlude content by default. In your `MainActivity.cs`:

```csharp
protected override void OnCreate(Bundle? savedInstanceState)
{
    base.OnCreate(savedInstanceState);

    // Draw behind system bars
    WindowCompat.SetDecorFitsSystemWindows(Window, false);

    // Make bars transparent so the image shows through
    Window.SetStatusBarColor(Android.Graphics.Color.Transparent);
    Window.SetNavigationBarColor(Android.Graphics.Color.Transparent);
}
```

You'll need the `Xamarin.AndroidX.Core` NuGet (usually already referenced) for `WindowCompat`.

**3. Ensure the Image itself fills the space:**

```xml
<Image Source="photo.jpg"
       Aspect="AspectFill"
       VerticalOptions="Fill"
       HorizontalOptions="Fill" />
```

On **iOS**, `SafeAreaEdges="None"` is usually sufficient — iOS doesn't need extra platform code. If you still see gaps, check for any `Margin` or `Padding` on the page or its parent layout.
data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about how to make content go edge-to-edge in a .NET MAUI app, going behind the status bar and navigation bar. They've tried `SafeAreaEdges="None"` on the ContentPage but still see padding.

This is a common .NET MAUI question. Let me think about what I know:

In .NET MAUI, to achieve true edge-to-edge content:

1. `SafeAreaEdges` - This property was introduced but it controls where safe area insets are applied. Setting it to `None` should remove safe area padding.

2. However, there are additional things needed:
   - On Android, you need to opt into edge-to-edge mode. Starting with Android 15 (API 35), apps are edge-to-edge by default. But for earlier versions, you may need to set it up.
   - On iOS, the safe area insets are managed by the system.

The real issue is often that the `Shell` or `NavigationPage` is adding its own chrome, and on Android the system bars aren't transparent by default. I'll likely need to suggest platform-specific configuration to handle this properly.

For .NET MAUI 8 and beyond, the user should set `SafeAreaEdges="None"` on the page—which they already did—but they also need to ensure edge-to-edge rendering is enabled on Android and make the status and navigation bars transparent. Though I should note that .NET MAUI's approach to edge-to-edge has evolved, especially with .NET 9+. The `SafeAreaEdges` property alone might not be sufficient; on iOS in particular, they may need to check that the page content doesn't have implicit margins, and there could be other configuration needed depending on their shell or navigation setup. On iOS, setting `SafeAreaEdges="None"` on the Image should resolve the issue, though I need to make sure the Image itself doesn't have any margins adding extra space.
